transcript: law that goes into effect next year forbids workers from being penalized or retaliated against by their employers for asking their co-workers about their pay or encouraging others they work with to ask for a raise. And speaking of wages, California's minimum wage will increase to $16 an hour for all employees January 1st. That's 50 cents more than the required minimum last year. Other laws passed require fast food workers to be paid a minimum wage of $20 an hour. And the state's lowest paid health care workers
